title: Vandy - Exercise Stress Test
author: Louise Lantier

steps_raw.instruction: Mice are familiarized with the treadmill two days prior to the experiment by exercising them for 10 minutes at 10m/min.

On the day of the experiment, the mouse is placed in the stopped treadmill for 45 min to acclimatize and the Oxymax system is started. The treadmill is equipped with a shock pad. Shocks are set at 1.5mA, 200ms pulses, 4Hz.
After 45 min, basal measurements are made for 15 min in the stopped treadmill.
After 3 min, speed is increased by 4m/min.

Every three minutes thereafter, speed is increased by 4m/min until mouse has reached exhaustion (table 1). Exhaustion is defined as the point at which the mouse remains on the shock pad for 5 continuous seconds. Table 1
Once exhaustion is reached, the mouse is left 30 min in the stopped treadmill to recover.
